sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this arcade-style flight shooter

special_mechanics.name: No Inertia Flight
special_mechanics.description: Player's craft has no inertia, meaning course can be changed instantly and can fly at low speeds without falling

special_mechanics.name: Afterburners
special_mechanics.description: Powerful afterburners allow craft to move at very high speed for evasion, but sacrifices ability to return fire temporarily

special_mechanics.name: Collision Tolerance
special_mechanics.description: Craft is able to survive some hits and collisions with terrain, including tunnels

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative. Terminal Velocity is a 1995 arcade-style flight combat game, not a traditional platformer with jump mechanics. The documentation describes gameplay characteristics (no inertia, instant direction change, afterburners) but does not provide specific physics values for movement parameters, gravity, or jump heights. The game emphasizes fast action and simplified controls compared to flight simulators of the time.
